about.title: About · Lucas Mujica
about.description: The person behind the builds. Senior Webflow & front-end developer, and chess player, traveler, singer, and serial optimizer off the clock.

Hero.sub: 5+ years deep. I build high-performing, component-driven sites marketing teams and small businesses can own, scale and maintain long after launch.

Work.heading: Builds I'm *proud* to put my name on.
Work.lede: Each one is built to be owned: a component system the client's team can run, scale and maintain, with no developer on call.
Work.allLede: Every build in one place: deep-dive case studies plus the rest of the shelf.

About.heading: A senior Webflow developer who *ships*.
About.lede: Five-plus years and 100+ projects of production-grade Webflow. Component-first builds with CMS architecture that marketing teams and small businesses actually enjoy running. Client-First certified, GSAP for the motion, and React / Next.js when a build calls for it. Fast on day one, still easy to edit a year later.

AboutPage.heading: The person *behind the builds*.
AboutPage.lede: I'm Lucas, a senior Webflow & front-end developer. But there's more to the story than the work, so here's the rest of it.
intro: I build websites for a living, but what I actually love is systems, the kind that turn something messy into something that just *works*. That instinct shows up everywhere: in a component library a client's team can run on their own, in the openings I study before a tournament, in how I plan a trip down to the last train.

Off the clock, I'm usually doing one of four things: pushing pawns, chasing a ball across the clay, hopping a flight somewhere new, or singing badly on purpose. None of it is unrelated. It's all the same curiosity, pointed in different directions.
AboutPage.journeyLede: The teams and studios I've shipped work with.

journey.note: Built scalable Webflow sites for global startups in a 4-person dev team, owning project timelines, client communication, QA, and reusable CMS structures.

Delivered 80+ production sites for international clients: standardizing Client-First, building reusable symbol/utility systems and advanced GSAP interactions, and automating reporting with Make & Zapier to save the marketing team 10+ hrs a week.

Co-founded a Webflow studio focused on high-complexity builds: end-to-end CMS architecture, reusable component libraries, and Make.com automation that lets content teams run their sites without a developer on call.
Building enterprise-grade Webflow sites for confidential tech clients: CMS architecture, custom animations, localization workflows, and front-end performance, alongside design and PM teams.

AboutPage.facetsLede: The things that keep me curious, and quietly make me better at the work.
AboutPage.chessTitle: Chess, since I was a kid
AboutPage.chessBody: My oldest obsession. I was runner-up in the Buenos Aires City under-10 championship at age 8. I drifted for a while, then came back all-in in 2021. Now I play individual tournaments year-round and even travel to compete with a team. It's where I train the patience and pattern-reading the work runs on too.
AboutPage.chessPhotoAlt: Lucas studying the board at a chess tournament
AboutPage.travelBody: New countries, new cultures, and the small details that make each place tick.
AboutPage.travelPhotoAlt: Lucas above the lakes and mountains of Bariloche, Argentina
AboutPage.gotBody: Split, Croatia. And yes, I claimed the throne.
AboutPage.gotPhotoAlt: Lucas on the Game of Thrones Iron Throne replica in Split
AboutPage.singingBody: I took singing lessons and never really stopped. It lives on a little TikTok, just for the joy of it.
AboutPage.singingPhotoAlt: Lucas at the keyboard with a mate
AboutPage.tennisBody: When I'm not over a chessboard, I'm chasing a ball on clay.
AboutPage.tennisPhotoAlt: Lucas playing tennis on a clay court

AboutPage.ctaHeading: Like how I think? Let's *build something*.
AboutPage.ctaLede: Whether it's a site, a migration, or a system that needs untangling, I'd love to hear about it.

Stack.headingPre: The right tool for the job,
Stack.lede: Everything here is chosen for speed, maintainability, and shipping fast without cutting corners.

Process.heading: How I *work*.
Process.lede: No mystery, no bloated decks. Just the three phases behind every site I ship.

steps.title: Strategy before pixels
steps.body: We nail the goal and what “done” means before I open the canvas. Skip this and you're just decorating guesses.

steps.title: Built to scale, not to impress
steps.body: Component-first, performance-first. Real motion where it earns its place. Zero bloat, scalable from day one.

steps.title: Launch, then earn it
steps.body: Shipping is the start, not the finish. I tune against real data until the numbers move. Opinions don't get a vote.

Testimonials.heading: Don't take my *word* for it.
Testimonials.lede: Proven results, from the people who shipped alongside me.

Contact.heading: Ready to build.
Contact.lead: A few quick details so I can scope it before we even talk, or just email me directly.
Contact.successBody: I'll get back to you with next steps within a day.
Contact.errorBody: Something went wrong on my end. Email me directly and I'll jump on it.

NotFound.heading: This page took a *detour*.
NotFound.body: The link's broken or the page moved. Let's get you back to the work.
